sections.body_he: Fictional Larkmont lies far from city lights, so its skies are almost free of light pollution (Bortle 3 in the demo).
On moonless nights the core of the Milky Way appears as a glowing band, and hundreds of stars are visible to the naked eye. All the data here is a demonstration.
From spring to autumn the galactic core rides high in the south; in winter the evening skies are clear and cold.
